briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 17, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Severe Impacts and Warnings of Domestic Climate Change
sections.content: Today, alarm bells have rung domestically regarding the severe risks posed by climate change. Due to climate change, the wildfire risk period has increased by up to 3.2 times, and the monsoon effect is collapsing, effectively making wildfires a year-round threat. This calls for the improvement of outdated policy systems that have lingered in the past [1, 9, 120]. Furthermore, research results have repeatedly reported that reduced physical activity and lack of exercise due to heatwaves could increase the possibility of up to 700,000 premature deaths annually by 2050, thereby shortening life expectancy [2, 7, 11, 16, 19]. This demonstrates the direct threat that global heating poses to our daily lives and health.

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts and Adaptation Achievements
sections.content: Amidst the climate crisis, various efforts and achievements towards realizing domestic carbon neutrality continue. Chungcheongnam-do Province, following its declaration as a 'Carbon Neutral Economic Special Province,' has reduced 1.08 million tons of greenhouse gases over three years, achieving the effect of planting 100 million trees, and has activated a command center to achieve its 2045 carbon neutrality goal [22, 30, 37, 40, 41, 46, 53, 55, 58, 59, 61, 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 70, 71, 76]. Several local governments, including Jeonju City and Dobong-gu, are leading carbon neutrality and resource circulation through initiatives such as mandatory eco-friendly banners, energy-saving reward services, and the expansion of resident-participatory environmental education [29, 34, 47, 54, 62, 69, 73, 75]. Furthermore, the Gyeonggi Future Generation Foundation operates education linking diet and climate change, and Samcheok City is actively soliciting citizen-participatory ideas for carbon neutrality, demonstrating vibrant efforts through citizen engagement [4, 10, 27, 38, 39, 48, 49, 52, 57, 72, 74, 77]. Additionally, the benefits of climate change adaptation investments have been shown to outweigh the costs, leading to a favorable issuance of adaptation bonds. Efforts for climate justice are also becoming visible, with the heatwave warning system being reorganized in response to the era of climate crisis and persons with disabilities officially participating in the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change [5, 6, 8, 17].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Challenges
sections.content: Domestically, policies and investments to accelerate the renewable energy transition are being actively discussed. The government is exploring reforms to the renewable energy supply system by shifting offshore wind development to a government-led 'planned site' approach [12, 98]. Large-scale renewable energy projects are underway, such as the designation of offshore wind power complex sites in Jeollanam-do (7.3 GW) and Gunsan (10.7 trillion KRW) [106, 107, 108, 112, 113, 116]. Woori Financial Group and Hana Financial Group have announced plans to expand renewable energy investments by creating a regional infrastructure fund totaling 500 billion KRW [83, 85, 89, 90, 93, 94, 95, 100, 103, 104, 114, 117]. Uiwang Urban Corporation's acquisition of low-carbon certification and efforts to cultivate new and renewable energy talent are also continuing [84, 91]. However, challenges that need to be addressed during the renewable energy transition are also being raised, including the issue of insufficient next-generation power grids and the paradoxical increase in carbon emissions from heat pumps during cold waves [101, 115].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Awareness and Response to the Climate Crisis
sections.content: The international community also faces the widespread impacts of climate change. One study warned that reduced physical activity due to global warming could contribute to millions of additional premature deaths worldwide by 2050, raising the possibility that climate change will lead to more fatalities and decreased productivity [123, 124, 130, 142, 144, 145]. In Minnesota, it was reported that the state could pay billions of dollars annually if it fails to adapt to climate change, and negative impacts have also been confirmed in the UK housing market and on Japanese salmon populations [121, 132, 136]. Furthermore, extreme weather is being observed globally, with rain falling more intensely and abnormal weather phenomena becoming more frequent due to climate change [127, 129, 133, 134]. The impact of climate change extends even to the sports sector, to the extent that the schedule for the Paralympics may need to be reconsidered [135].

briefing.closing: As we have reviewed in today's briefing, climate change poses a serious threat both domestically and internationally. However, at the same time, diverse efforts and investments are actively underway to overcome these challenges. This is a critical time when sustained attention and action become even more important. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: On the 24th of last month, a helicopter was extinguishing a wildfire that had broken out the previous day in Miryang City, Gyeongsangnam-do. Reporter Jeong Hyo-jin reports that a research result has been published stating that due to climate change, the risk of wildfires, which was previously limited to spring, is now extending to winter in most parts of the country, leading to virtually 'year-round' wildfires. Even under the Paris Agreement's 1.5-degree rise scenario, 163 days a year were identified as high-risk periods for wildfires. Commissioned by the international environmental organization Greenpeace...

summary.phenomenon: On the 24th of last month, a helicopter extinguished a wildfire that had occurred the previous day in Miryang City, Gyeongsangnam-do. Research results have also been published indicating that the risk of wildfires, previously confined to spring, is now extending to winter in most parts of the country.
summary.cause: This is due to rising temperatures caused by climate change, which extends the wildfire risk period and eliminates the wildfire off-season.
summary.outlook: If temperatures rise further, wildfires could occur for 336 days a year. Even under the Paris Agreement's 1.5-degree rise scenario, 163 days a year are projected to be high-risk periods for wildfires, indicating a virtual 'year-round' wildfire season.
